nodes.context: Mexican drug cartel based in Ciudad Juárez, Chihuahua, involved in drug trafficking and violence.
Mexican drug cartel, rival and former partner of Juárez Cartel.
Armed wing and leading faction of the Juárez Cartel, composed of corrupt police officers.
Street gang used by Juárez Cartel to attack enemies, operates in Mexico and US.
Mexican drug cartel, former ally of Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel, loosely allied with Juárez Cartel since 2010.
Mexican drug cartel, loosely allied with Juárez Cartel since 2010.
Mexican drug cartel, rival of Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel, now disbanded, rival of Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel, rival of Juárez Cartel and allied with Nuevo Cartel de Juárez.
Splinter group allied with CJNG, wrestled control from original Juárez Cartel by 2020.
Powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel, built presence in Ciudad Juárez.
Street gang, rival of Juárez Cartel.
Drug trafficking group, rival of Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel, rival of Juárez Cartel.
Defunct Mexican drug cartel, former ally of Juárez Cartel.
Defunct Colombian drug cartel, former ally of Juárez Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is one of several drug trafficking organizations that have been known to decapitate their rivals, mutilate their corpses and dump them in public places to instill fear not only in the general public but also in local law enforcement and their rivals, the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Sinaloa Cartel are rivals, with Juárez using violence to instill fear in Sinaloa.

edges.evidence_quote: The Juárez Cartel has an armed wing known as La Línea, a Juárez street gang that usually performs the executions and is now the cartel’s most powerful and leading faction.
edges.context: La Línea is the armed wing and leading faction of the Juárez Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: It also uses the Barrio Azteca gang to attack its enemies.
edges.context: Juárez Cartel uses Barrio Azteca as an enforcement gang to attack enemies.

edges.evidence_quote: formed an alliance with other drug lords such as Ismael "Mayo" Zambada in Sinaloa and Baja California, the Beltrán Leyva brothers in Monterrey
edges.context: Juárez Cartel formed an alliance with the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Since 2007, the Juárez Cartel has been locked in a vicious battle with its former partner, the Sinaloa Cartel, for control of Juárez.
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Sinaloa Cartel have been in a violent conflict since 2007.

edges.evidence_quote: Vicente Carrillo Fuentes then formed a partnership with Juan José Esparragoza Moreno ... and formed an alliance with other drug lords such as Ismael "Mayo" Zambada in Sinaloa and Baja California ... and Joaquín "El Chapo" Guzmán in Nayarit, Sinaloa, and Tamaulipas.
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Sinaloa Cartel were formerly allied as part of the Golden Triangle Alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: Since March 2010, it is alleged that the major cartels have aligned into two loosely allied factions, one integrated by the Juárez Cartel, the Tijuana Cartel, Los Zetas, and the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Tijuana Cartel are loosely allied since March 2010.

edges.evidence_quote: Since March 2010, it is alleged that the major cartels have aligned into two loosely allied factions, one integrated by the Juárez Cartel, the Tijuana Cartel, Los Zetas, and the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Los Zetas are loosely allied since March 2010.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Gulf Cartel are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and La Familia Michoacana are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Jalisco New Generation Cartel are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and MS-13 are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Gente Nueva are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Knights Templar Cartel are rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Sinaloa Cartel, MS-13, Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Gente Nueva, Knights Templar Cartel, Gulf Cartel, La Familia Michoacana, Tijuana Cartel, Mexican government, U.S. government
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Tijuana Cartel are rivals (despite earlier alliance).

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Línea (lead faction), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct), Barrio Azteca, Los Zetas, Oaxaca Cartel (defunct), Cali Cartel (defunct)
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Oaxaca Cartel were allies.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Línea (lead faction), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct), Barrio Azteca, Los Zetas, Oaxaca Cartel (defunct), Cali Cartel (defunct)
edges.context: Juárez Cartel and Cali Cartel were allies.

edges.evidence_quote: By 2020, the Nuevo Cartel de Juárez, allied with the CJNG, began to wrestle control away from the original Juárez Cartel.
edges.context: Nuevo Cartel de Juárez is allied with CJNG.

edges.evidence_quote: By 2020, the Nuevo Cartel de Juárez, allied with the CJNG, began to wrestle control away from the original Juárez Cartel.
edges.context: Nuevo Cartel de Juárez is in conflict with the original Juárez Cartel.

edges.evidence_quote: Los Salazar, a powerful cell of the Sinaloa Cartel, had by this point managed to build a significant presence in Ciudad Juárez as well.
edges.context: Los Salazar is a cell of the Sinaloa Cartel.
